rows.notes: Top-centre title word looks like 'Receipta' (handwriting looped). Clear that this is a page heading; no numeric columns present. Some letters ambiguous (e.g. final 'a'/'e').

Large header text across top left — appears to record arrears and a date (Novr. 2. 1760). Several letters are smudged and spelling is uncertain; I preserved visible letter shapes as best read ('Arreargia reciepnda'). Low confidence for exact spelling but clear this is a header with date.

rows.description: Longwittham pro Prin: an: ad fest Michl. 1760
rows.notes: Description moderately legible though some letters uncertain ('Longwittham' is best reading). Pound/shilling/pence columns are clearly aligned; pounds '26', shillings '0', pence '7' are crisp and valid.
Single-word place name clearly reads as 'Yardington'. Numeric columns clear: 18 pounds, 14 shillings, 0 pence.

rows.description: Lord olin Bigs name Taylor pro Teniis
rows.notes: Description is somewhat uncertain (several proper names; transcribed as seen). Monetary columns are clear: 12/18/10. Chose this reading though some letters in description are ambiguous.

rows.description: Little Tees Quit Rents pro Frein
rows.notes: Description reads as 'Little Tees Quit Rents...' (handwriting fairly clear). Amount columns are well aligned: 10 pounds, 0 shillings, 6 pence.
Place/tenant names slightly uncertain but legible enough. Numeric columns clear: 9/14/11.
Description moderately legible (surname ending looks like 'Molineax'). Pence column clearly shows '2' with a small '3/4' mark; interpreted as three-farthings = 0.75. Columns alignment unambiguous.
Second similar line ('Ditto') with identical numeric pattern. Pence again shows '2' with a '3/4' sign; interpreted as 2¾d (0.75). Description short and readable.
Place name 'South Newington' clear; following words uncertain but transcribed as seen. Amount columns clear: 1/6/10.
Description partially unclear (transcribed to match visible letters). Monetary columns unambiguous: 3 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence.
Line begins with a dash (sub-item). Place name reading 'Munton' uncertain but plausible. Numbers 8/6/8 are clear in columns.
Description reads 'Minchin' (fairly clear). Pounds/shillings/pence show 1/12/2¾ — pence '2' with '3/4' mark; interpreted as 0.75 fraction. Column alignment consistent.
Clear place label 'South Newington Rect.' and a large round sum in the pounds column: 58/0/0. Appears to be an item rather than page-level final total.
Label 'Kidlington Rect.' readable; amount columns show a round 60/0/0. Description's final word uncertain ('Grien'), but numeric entry is clear.
Personal name reads as 'Lucy Eaton' clearly; amount 6/0/0 is clearly written in the columns.

Horizontal rule followed by a boxed total in the right columns: '230' pounds, '17' shillings, '8 1/2' pence. Pence clearly shows '8' and a half-penny sign (interpreted as 0.5). No description text next to these numbers.
Lower-section entry; description letters are faint/abbreviated — read as 'Sayer pro anno'. Numbers 1/3/4 in columns are legible but handwriting of description is less clear.
Line begins with dash; text 'pro Capon' visible. Pounds column blank/0, shillings '5', pence '0' clearly written.
Name unclear ('Hattale' best reading). Amounts 1/13/4 readable. Description letters somewhat indistinct which lowers confidence.
Proper name uncertain (transcribed as 'Ledwull'); numeric columns show 0/10/0 clearly. The initial pound column is blank/zero.
Short name 'Gurniar' (best reading) and a large round amount '100/0/0' clearly visible in the pounds column. Uncertainty only in exact spelling of name.
Description is poorly legible; 'Dockgate' and following words are guesses based on letter shapes. Amount 18/0/0 is clear in the columns.
Reads 'Kidlington vice' (place + 'vice'); amount 4/0/0 is clear. Minor uncertainty in suffix words.
Probably 'Millmount' or 'Muilmount' Rect. Amount 20/0/0 is clearly written. Some letters in the place name ambiguous.
Reads like 'Franklyn manor Hall' — letters for 'manor' and 'Hall' are suggested by shapes. Amount 1/13/0 clear. Description uncertainty reduces confidence.
Dash indicates sub-item 'Ditto Capons' (legible). Amount 0/5/0 plainly written.
Place name approximated ('Bensington Barnet' best reading). Numeric columns show 11/17/10 clearly. Some letters in description ambiguous.

rows.description: Cannock Waste at Geo? pro Frein:
rows.notes: Description is faint and abbreviated; 'Cannock Waste' plausible, with following words unclear. Amount 0/1/6 is legible. Low confidence in description spelling.
Final lower-section total at very bottom-right reads '159 9 0' (pounds/shillings/pence). No description text adjacent; numeric columns are clear.
